examples.title: FIFO Page Replacement Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates First-In-First-Out page replacement when a page fault occurs and all frames are occupied
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FIFO_Page_Replacement
INPUT: Page reference string R[], frames F[], frame_count n
BEGIN
  page_faults = 0
  front = 0  // Points to oldest page
  rear = n-1 // Points to newest page
  
  FOR each page p IN R[] DO
    IF p NOT IN F[] THEN
      // Page fault occurs
      page_faults = page_faults + 1
      
      IF frame_count < n THEN
        // Frame available
        F[frame_count] = p
        frame_count = frame_count + 1
      ELSE
        // All frames occupied - evict FIFO
        F[front] = p
        front = (front + 1) % n
      END IF
    END IF
  END FOR
  
  RETURN page_faults
END
examples.explanation: FIFO maintains a queue of pages in memory. When a page needs to be evicted, the page that has been in memory the longest (the front of the queue) is selected. This algorithm is simple but doesn't consider page usage patterns.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) per page reference, O(m) total where m is number of references

examples.title: LRU Page Replacement Implementation
examples.description: Python implementation of Least Recently Used page replacement using a counter
examples.code:
class LRUReplacer:
    def __init__(self, num_frames):
        self.frames = [None] * num_frames
        self.counters = [0] * num_frames  # Timestamp counter
        self.counter = 0
        self.frame_count = 0
    
    def access_page(self, page):
        # Check if page is already in memory
        if page in self.frames:
            idx = self.frames.index(page)
            self.counters[idx] = self.counter
            self.counter += 1
            return False  # No page fault
        
        # Page fault occurs
        if self.frame_count < len(self.frames):
            # Empty frame available
            self.frames[self.frame_count] = page
            self.counters[self.frame_count] = self.counter
            self.frame_count += 1
        else:
            # All frames occupied - find LRU page
            lru_idx = self.counters.index(min(self.counters))
            self.frames[lru_idx] = page
            self.counters[lru_idx] = self.counter
        
        self.counter += 1
        return True  # Page fault occurred
    
    def display_frames(self):
        print("Frames:", self.frames)

# Example usage
lru = LRUReplacer(3)
references = [1, 2, 3, 4, 1, 2, 5, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
total_faults = 0

for page in references:
    if lru.access_page(page):
        total_faults += 1
        print(f"Page {page}: Fault")
    else:
        print(f"Page {page}: Hit")

print(f"Total Page Faults: {total_faults}")
examples.explanation: LRU tracks when each page was last used by maintaining a counter. When replacement is needed, the page with the smallest counter (least recently used) is evicted. This better approximates the Optimal algorithm than FIFO.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) per access where n is number of frames

examples.title: Demand Paging Simulation
examples.description: C program demonstrating demand paging with page fault handling
examples.explanation: This C program simulates demand paging where pages are loaded into memory only when accessed (upon page fault). It demonstrates page table management, frame allocation, and simple FIFO replacement when all frames are occupied.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) per memory access on average (with TLB), O(n) worst case for page fault
examples.spaceComplexity: O(P + F) where P = page table size, F = number of frames
